description_long: This object resembles a leafy sea dragon, a marine crustacean with an elongated, slender body and numerous leaf-like appendages extending from its sides and back. The appendages are irregularly shaped and mimic seaweed, providing excellent camouflage. The body is segmented, with a distinct head featuring small, branching antennae and a narrow snout. The coloration is primarily olive green with hints of brown, giving it a natural, organic appearance. The legs and appendages are thin and delicate, with no visible claws, and the overall form is lightweight and airy. The surface appears hard and slightly rough, resembling the exoskeleton of a real crustacean.
description: A leafy sea dragon-like crustacean with olive green, leaf-shaped appendages and a segmented, elongated body. Its surface is hard and textured, with delicate, branch-like features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated body, numerous leaf-like appendages, and the branching antennae at the head.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow profile, symmetrical arrangement of appendages, and the central placement of the head and antennae.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, displaying the full length of the body and the spread of the leafy appendages.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tapering tail, the arrangement of appendages along the body, and the overall symmetry.
